title: Greek Bailout Referendum 2015: Public opinion on accepting the terms of the EU/IMF bailout package

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on pre-referendum announcement polling trends from Alco/Kapa Research
Estimated: Shift following the breakdown of negotiations in Brussels
Estimated: Impact of capital controls announcement
Estimated: Post-bank closure panic and government 'No' campaign momentum
Estimated: Final polling convergence toward referendum result

events.description: Prime Minister Alexis Tsipras announces a surprise referendum on bailout terms.
Greek government announces bank holidays and capital controls.
Greece misses IMF debt repayment deadline, officially defaulting.

agents.name: Syriza Party
agents.description: Governing party advocating for a 'No' vote to strengthen bargaining position.

agents.name: New Democracy
agents.description: Main opposition party advocating for a 'Yes' vote to keep Greece in the Eurozone.

agents.name: European Central Bank
agents.description: Restricted liquidity to Greek banks, pressuring the 'Yes' outcome.

agents.name: Urban Professional Cluster
agents.description: Demographic concerned with financial stability and Eurozone membership.

agents.name: Public Sector Workers
agents.description: Demographic heavily impacted by austerity measures.

agents.name: Greek Orthodox Church
agents.description: Remained largely neutral, calling for national unity.

notes: Pro-side = 'Yes' (accept bailout terms, keep Euro); Against-side = 'No' (reject bailout terms, anti-austerity). Polling data is estimated based on historical trends as high-frequency polling during this 10-day period was volatile and often contradictory.
